humanities_role_explanation: Uses graduate-level academic questions from arts and humanities disciplines (such as Art Studies, Musicology, Philosophy, History, and Literature) as test content to evaluate general LLM knowledge and reasoning capabilities.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: The benchmark is text-only. Multimodal inputs (like diagrams or images) are explicitly filtered out during the quality inspection phase to ensure a clean text-based QA format.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Graduate-level multiple-choice questions requiring domain-specific factual knowledge retrieval, text analysis, and complex reasoning across specialized academic disciplines.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: SuperGPQA scales LLM evaluation across 285 graduate disciplines spanning STEM, social sciences, and humanities.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates various SOTA generative models including DeepSeek-R1, OpenAI o1/o3-mini, GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Qwen2.5, Llama-3.1, and Gemini-2.0-flash.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary objective of the paper is to benchmark and analyze the performance capabilities of frontier LLMs.

arts_humanities_data.note: Includes substantial subsets for History (674 questions), Philosophy (347 questions), Literature and Arts (1,676 questions including Art Studies, Musicology, and Language/Literature).

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluated using accuracy (analyzed sample-wise, subfield-wise, field-wise, and discipline-wise).

published_after_march_2023.note: The paper is a preprint from March 2025.

english_language.note: The paper is written in English, and all benchmark questions are curated in or translated into English.

summary: SuperGPQA is a massive, graduate-level multiple-choice benchmark evaluating LLM capabilities across 285 specialized disciplines, including several key arts and humanities domains. Using a human-LLM collaborative filtering framework, the authors curated a dataset of 26,529 highly difficult questions designed to challenge state-of-the-art reasoning models.
key_findings: Reasoning models DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI's o1 achieved the highest accuracies (~61.82% and ~60.24% respectively), demonstrating significant remaining climbing space. Instruction tuning provides a massive performance boost over base models, and incorporating subfield context into evaluation prompts systematically benefits larger models.
